summary: Two-time Asian Cup champions under Julen Lopetegui, looking to redeem three home defeats as hosts in 2022. Build from the back toward talisman Akram Afif, with a heavy reliance on set pieces — a 4-2-3-1 that focuses on width but has few attacking solutions beyond Afif.
style: 4-2-3-1 / 4-4-2 — build from the back and progress to Afif, the main creative force; focus on the flanks but limited variety in attack. Drops into a mid-block to spring counters and goes direct when needed (most long balls per 90).

counterReliance.note: Mid-block to spring counters; direct, set-piece-reliant

strengths: Set-piece reliant — 42% of qualifying goals from dead balls (the two FK goals that beat the UAE 2-1 sealed qualification; 8 of 19 set-piece goals in rounds 3–4)
Akram Afif — creator, dribbler, finisher, and the set-piece delivery behind the dead-ball threat
Two-time Asian Cup winners with deep continuity in the squad
weaknesses: Defensive worries (negative goal difference in qualifying)

keyPlayers.name: Akram Afif
keyPlayers.note: Does everything — dribbles, passes, scores, and delivers the set pieces. 2019 & 2023 Asian Cup winner; 2023 Golden Boot and Player of the Tournament

tactics: Drop into a mid-block, build toward Afif, and lean on dead balls — the set-piece threat is the genuine edge. Their progression to the knockouts likely hinges on the final group game against Bosnia.
notes: Only previous World Cup appearance was as hosts in 2022, losing all three group games.
sources: The Athletic — 2026 World Cup tactical guide, Group B (5 Jun 2026)
John Walker (@johnwalker_1986) — pre-tournament tactical scouting, May–Jun 2026
